title: Fenazaquin and indoxacarb in a product for killing insects, especially mosquitoes
abstract: Fenazaquin and Indoxacarbin a product and method against insects, especially mosquitoes for killing insects, especially adult mosquitoes, a substrate, for example fabric,is provided with Fenazaquin and Indoxacarb.
claims: Claims 1. A method of killing insects, the method comprising providing a non-living substrate with Indoxacarb, IDX, and Fenazaquin, FNQ, on its surface and exposing the insect to the Indoxacarb and Fenazaquin by contact with the surface. 2. A method according to claim 1, wherein the insect is an adult mosquito. 3. A method according claim 2, the method comprising providing the IDX and the FNQ on the surface with a concentration ratio IDX: FNQ between the concentration of IDX and the concentration of FNQ, adjusting the concentration ratio IDX: FNQ for synergistic or additive lethal action on the adult mosquito but not antagonistic action, and exposing the adult mosquito to the IDX and FNQ in this synergistic concentration ratio by contact with the surface. 4. A method according to claim 2, wherein the concentration ratio of IDX: FNQ is less than 2 : 1. 5. A method according to claim 2, wherein the concentration ratio of IDX: FNQ is less than 1 : 1. 6. A method according to any preceding claim, the method comprising coating the surface of the substrate with an impregnation formulation comprising IDX, FNQ and an impregnation binder and causing migration of IDX and FNQ to the surface of the impregnation formulation; or wherein substrate is a thermoplastic polymer and the method comprises melting the polymer, mixing IDX and FNQ into the molten polymer and extruding or molding the polymer as part of the production of the substrate and causing migration of IDX and FNQ to the surface of the polymer. 7. A method according to any preceding claim, comprising providing the substrate as a fabric. 8. A substrate that is non-living and comprises a surface for contact with an in- sect, the surface comprising a combination of Indoxacarb, IDX, and Fenazaquin, FNQ. 9. A substrate according to claim 8, wherein the IDX and FNQ are provided on the surface with a concentration ratio IDX: FNQ between the concentration of IDX and the concentration of FNQ for synergistic or additive lethal but not antagonistic effect on adult mosquitoes. 10. A substrate according to claim 9, wherein the weight concentration ratio IDX: FNQ is less than 2 : 1. 11. A substrate for a method according to claim 9, wherein the weight con- centration ratio IDX: FNQ is less than 1 : 1. 12. A substrate according to anyone of the claims 8-11, wherein the substrate is a thermoplastic polymer having melt-incorporated therein IDX and FNQ and being configured for migration of IDX and FNQ to the surface of the polymer. 13. A substrate according to claim 12, wherein concentration of FNQ in the polymer is in the range of 0.5 and 5 wt%. 14. A substrate according to claim 12 or 13, wherein, and the concentration of IDX is in the range of 0.5 and 5 g wt%. 15. A substrate according to anyone of the claims 12-14 , wherein the substrate is an extruded polylolefin monofilament yarn. 16. A substrate according to anyone of the claims 8-11, the substrate comprising a coating forming a surface on the substrate, the coating comprising the combination of IDX and FNQ. 17. A substrate according to claim 16, wherein the FNQ is present in the coating as a first portion and a second portion, the first portion being provided in particular form and the second portion being provided in dissolved form on a supporting micro-particle. 18. A substrate according to claim 16 or 17, wherein the substrate is a multi- filament polypropylene yarn with 50-100 Denier, the multifilament yarn comprising 10-100 monofilaments. 19. A substrate according to claim 15 or 18, wherein the yarn is part of a fabric. 20. A substrate according to claim 19, wherein the fabric is a mosquito net. 21. A method for producing a substrate according to claim 16, wherein the method comprises providing a substrate and coating it with an impregnation formulation containing FNQ and IDX; wherein the impregnation formulation is provided by dividing the FNQ into a first portion and a second portion, providing a first aqueous intermediate formulation with the first portion and a second intermediate formulation with the second portion, adding the IDX to the first or second intermediate formulation, and mixing the first and the second intermediate formulation with the addition of an impregnation binder to form the impregnation formulation; wherein the first intermediate formulation is provided by mixing the first portion of FNQ with water. 22. A method for producing a substrate according to claim 21, wherein the second intermediate formulation is provided by adding porous micro-particles as support for the FNQ in or on the micro-particles. 23. A method according to claim 22, wherein the porous micro-particles are of the type ES-4â¢ marketed by Amcol, CAS# 25777-71-3, the micro-particles having a size in the range of 10-50 microns. 24. A method according to anyone of the claims 21-23, wherein the concentration ratio between the concentration of IDX and the concentration of FNQ in the impregnation formulation is 2 : 1. 25. A method according to claim 24, wherein the concentration ratio between the concentration of IDX and the concentration of FNQ in the impregnation formulation is less than 1 : 1.
